metadata.semantic_description:
Sex: Other
Competitive Agreeableness (32nd pct) — Competitive, skeptical, challenging
Moderate Conscientiousness (46th pct)
Highly extraverted Extraversion (84th pct) — Extraverted, outgoing, energetic
Anxious Neuroticism (77th pct) — Neurotic, anxious, emotionally reactive
Open Openness to Experience (66th pct) — Open, curious, imaginative, creative
  Low Tender-Mindedness (30th pct) — Tough-minded, unsympathetic, objective
  Moderate Achievement Striving (50th pct)
  Moderate Self-Discipline (41st pct)
  Very High Activity Level (99th pct) — Fast-paced, energetic, busy
  High Excitement Seeking (74th pct) — Thrill-seeking, excitement-hungry
  High Positive Emotions (74th pct) — Joyful, enthusiastic, optimistic
  High Anger / Hostility (68th pct) — Easily angered, irritable, hostile
  High Self-Consciousness (64th pct) — Self-conscious, shy, easily embarrassed
  Highly neurotic Neuroticism (86th pct) — Neurotic, anxious, emotionally reactive
  Low Chronological Age (36th pct)

message.reasoning:
We need to generate a baseline opinion score for the specific policy item: "Trust_In_Mainstream_Journalism" (part of Information_Integrity_And_Platforms). The profile describes a person (sex: Other, age 36, big five traits). Key traits: low agreeableness (especially trust at 25.7th percentile, compliance low, straightforwardness low), high extraversion (very high activity level, gregariousness, warmth), high neuroticism (anxiety high, anger/hostility high, stress vulnerability high), moderate-high openness, moderate conscientiousness. The semantic description highlights: competitive, skeptical, challenging, anxious, emotionally reactive, tough-minded, objective, fast-paced, excitement-seeking, joyful but easily angered, self-conscious.

For trust in mainstream journalism, this person likely has low trust given low agreeableness (low trust facet), high neuroticism (anxiety, anger), and low compliance/straightforwardness suggests skepticism towards authority and institutions. High openness might make them open to alternative information sources. High extraversion might mean they are engaged and talkative, but that doesn't necessarily increase trust in mainstream journalism. Given competitive and challenging nature, they may be critical of mainstream narratives. However, they are also high in positive emotions and warmth, which might soften extreme distrust. Also, they have moderate conscientiousness, so they may be somewhat deliberative but not overly rigid.

Thus, I expect a moderately negative score, but not extremely so (since they are not entirely anti-establishment based on moderate openness values and moderate conscientiousness). The trust facet is exceptionally low (25.7), which is a strong signal for distrust. But compliance is very low (18.2), indicating they don't easily defer. Straightforwardness low suggests they may be cunning or skeptical. Overall, I'd place them around -500 to -600, clear opposition. But need to avoid round numbers. Let's say -537, indicating clear opposition with some nuance. Confidence should reflect the strength of the profile signals. I'll set confidence at 0.78 since the low trust facet is very specific.

Reasoning: Low trust and compliance facets of agreeableness combined with high neuroticism (anxiety, anger) foster skepticism toward mainstream institutions, but high extraversion and openness prevent outright cynical rejection, resulting in clear opposition with moderate intensity.
